name: Sunnyland Slim
abstract: Albert Luandrew (September 5, 1906 – March 17, 1995), known as Sunnyland Slim, was an American blues pianist born in the Mississippi Delta and moved to Chicago, helping to make that city a center of postwar blues. Chicago broadcaster and writer Studs Terkel said Sunnyland Slim was "a living piece of our folk history, gallantly and eloque…

concerts.label: Boogie n' Blues at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: I just did a piece for the Wall Street Journal on a 46-year-old guy named Barrelhouse Chuck Goering, who is half Cherokee, who learned at the hands of Sunnyland Slim
about_quotes.doc_title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Ann Rabson
about_quotes.evidence: people like Sunnyland Slim, have all taken me under their wing, and you know they don’t do things for me, but they show me things and they encourage me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ann Rabson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 21, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Ousley
about_quotes.evidence: In fact I worked with Sunnyland and Memphis Slim, Roosevelt Sykes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Ousley interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: In fact, Sunnyland Slim, Memphis Slim, in fact I just finished an album last year with Etta James...
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995
